name: Brutalist
description: Raw, unapologetic, aggressively structural. Heavy borders, monolithic blocks, intentional visual tension. No polish—maximum impact.

layout.whitespace: used as a weapon, not comfort—either zero or extreme

typography.font_category: display-sans or grotesque (Impact, Anton, Space Grotesk)
typography.scale: extreme contrast—hero text 96-120px, body 14-16px, no gradual ramp
typography.weight: 900 for headings, 400 for body, nothing in between
typography.line_height: tight (1.0–1.1) for headings, normal (1.5) for body

shape.borders: 2-4px solid borders, often mismatched or offset

interaction.hover: color inversion or hard translate (transform: translate(-4px, -4px))
interaction.focus: 3px solid outline, high-contrast color, no border-radius
interaction.motion: instant—no easing, snappy state changes only
interaction.animation: avoid; if used, 0ms transition or abrupt toggle

dos: Break the grid deliberately and visibly
Use overlapping elements to create depth without shadows
Make interactive states feel violent and immediate
Expose structure—show the grid lines, the columns
donts: Do not add gradients or glassmorphism effects
Do not round corners
Do not use subtle transitions or ease-in-out animations
Do not use photographic backgrounds—flat color only
Do not use more than 3 colors total

name: Glassmorphism
description: Frosted glass panels floating over vivid gradient backgrounds. Translucent surfaces, blur effects, and layered depth define this ethereal aesthetic.

layout.grid: loose 4-8 column card grid with generous gutters (32-48px)
layout.stack: layered z-axis—cards float above background content

typography.weight: 300-500 range preferred; avoid heavy weights on glass panels
typography.line_height: relaxed (1.6–1.7) for body to maximize readability on blur

interaction.focus: luminous glow ring, 2px + 2px offset rgba ring
interaction.motion: smooth 200-300ms ease-out on all state transitions
interaction.animation: subtle float (translateY -2px on hover), gentle scale (1.01-1.02)

dos: Always place panels over a gradient or image background
Use backdrop-filter: blur(10-20px) on all panels
Layer multiple glass panels at different opacities for depth
Ensure text has sufficient contrast despite translucency (4.5:1 minimum)
donts: Do not use on solid color backgrounds—the effect disappears
Do not use more than 30% opacity on text containers
Do not mix glassmorphism with brutalist sharp edges
Do not stack more than 3 layers of glass or blur accumulates
Do not use dark text on dark blurred backgrounds

name: Neo-Brutalism
description: Brutalism upgraded with playful flat colors. Thick offset shadows, chunky borders, and saturated primaries create a bold, approachable energy without losing rawness.

layout.stack: horizontal card rows, staggered for visual rhythm
layout.whitespace: moderate; enough to breathe but cards dominate the viewport

typography.font_category: rounded-sans or quirky grotesque (DM Mono, Plus Jakarta Sans, Syne)
typography.transform: Mixed case—sentence or title, not all-caps

shape.shadows: 4-8px hard offset box-shadow in dark color (no blur: 4px 4px 0 #000)

interaction.hover: shadow collapses (translate to match shadow offset), brief scale up 1.02
interaction.motion: 100-150ms ease for shadow/translate, snappy but not instant

dos: Use 2-3 bold flat saturated colors (yellow, coral, teal, lime) as accents
Apply offset box-shadows consistently as the primary depth signal
Make buttons chunky and satisfying to click
Use illustration or simple iconography to reinforce playfulness
donts: Do not use gradients or transparency
Do not use more than 3 saturated accent colors
Do not soften shadows with blur—hard offset only
Do not use thin typography—weights below 600 for headings
Do not mix this with minimalist negative-space heavy layouts

name: Editorial
description: Typographic-first design inspired by print publications. Dense grids, mixed typefaces, strong baseline rhythm, and careful hierarchy make content the star.

layout.stack: horizontal reading flow; sidebar + main column standard
layout.density: high—content fills the page, minimal decorative whitespace
layout.whitespace: used as punctuation: gutters between articles, not within

typography.font_category: serif headline + humanist-sans body (Playfair Display + Source Sans, Cormorant + Inter)
typography.weight: bold/black for headlines, regular for body, italic for pull quotes
typography.transform: title case for headlines, sentence case for body
typography.line_height: tight headlines (1.0-1.15), generous body (1.65-1.75) for readability

shape.borders: thin rule lines (1px) as section dividers, horizontal only
shape.shadows: none—content hierarchy through typography, not elevation

interaction.hover: underline reveal on links, color shift from muted to accent
interaction.focus: thin high-contrast underline or outline, stays typographic
interaction.motion: minimal—200ms ease on color transitions only
interaction.animation: avoid decorative animation; content is the motion

dos: Establish and maintain a strict baseline grid (8px or 24px)
Use dropcaps, pull quotes, and ruled lines as layout accents
Pair a expressive serif headline font with a neutral sans body font
Let long-form content breathe with appropriate leading
donts: Do not use card-based layouts for article content
Do not use decorative icons or illustration in the primary flow
Do not center-align body text—left-align always
Do not use rounded buttons—text links or understated bordered buttons only
Do not use dark-mode gradients—this style is fundamentally print-inspired

name: Minimal Zen
description: Ultra-clean, silence-first design. Extreme whitespace, restrained typography, and single accent color. Every element earns its place or is removed.

layout.grid: single-column or 2-column max; central content column 640-720px max-width
layout.stack: vertical, generous vertical rhythm (64-96px between sections)
layout.density: very low—each element is isolated with space on all sides
layout.whitespace: the primary design element; at least 50% of the viewport is empty

typography.font_category: geometric-sans or Japanese-influenced sans (Helvetica, Neue Haas, Satoshi, Noto Sans)
typography.weight: 300-400 only; avoid bold except for single accent word
typography.transform: lowercase preferred or sentence case; no all-caps

shape.radius: 0px or very large (full pill: 999px) — binary choice, not in-between
shape.borders: single 1px hairline border as a design accent, used sparingly

interaction.hover: opacity reduction (0.6) or single color change—never movement
interaction.focus: visible 1px outline with 4px offset; accessibility without loudness
interaction.animation: avoid entirely; if needed, opacity fade only

dos: Remove every element that is not essential to the primary action
Use a single brand color as an accent only (sparingly, 5% of the page)
Obsess over text spacing: letter-spacing, word-spacing, line-height
Asymmetric layouts work; left-offset text on large whitespace canvas
donts: Do not use images as decorative elements
Do not use more than 2 font families
Do not use gradients, shadows, or visual noise
Do not add navigation items that aren't essential
Do not use color for more than one purpose (link color = link color only)

name: Data Dense
description: Information architecture optimized for analysts and power users. Tables, charts, status badges, and sidebar navigation dominate. Every pixel carries data.

layout.grid: fixed sidebar (240-280px) + main content area; tables and panels fill the main area
layout.stack: horizontal primary (sidebar + content), vertical within content sections
layout.density: very high—8-12px padding in cells, 16px between sections
layout.whitespace: minimal; used only to separate data groups, not for aesthetics

typography.font_category: monospace for data values + compact-sans for labels (JetBrains Mono, IBM Plex Mono + IBM Plex Sans)
typography.weight: 400 for data, 500-600 for labels, 700 for section headers only
typography.transform: sentence case for labels, exact case for data values

shape.radius: 2-4px on badges and chips; 0px on tables and panels
shape.borders: 1px solid borders everywhere—table rows, panel edges, input fields
shape.shadows: very subtle (0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)) on floating panels only

interaction.hover: row highlight (2% darker bg), sticky column reveal
interaction.focus: 2px inset outline; keyboard navigation must be fully supported
interaction.motion: instant or 100ms max—data apps feel laggy with animation
interaction.animation: loading spinners only; no decorative motion

dos: Right-align numeric columns, left-align text columns
Use status badge chips (rounded pill) for state: success/error/warning/info
Provide sticky headers on scrollable tables
Use condensed vertical spacing consistently—every pixel counts
donts: Do not use card grids for tabular data
Do not add decorative illustrations or hero images
Do not use animation for data updates—highlight the changed cell instead
Do not use fonts below 11px
Do not round table cell corners or add card shadows to data rows

name: Soft UI (Neumorphism)
description: Monochromatic surfaces with subtle extruded/inset shadows creating tactile depth. Buttons look pressed, not clicked. Materials feel solid and touchable.

layout.stack: centered content blocks with ample padding (48-64px)
layout.density: low-medium; components need space to show their shadow depth
layout.whitespace: generous; the background IS the design element

typography.font_category: rounded-sans or clean humanist (Nunito, Poppins, Quicksand)

shape.radius: 12-20px on cards, 50% on circular controls, 8px on inputs
shape.borders: none—depth comes from shadow, not borders
shape.shadows: dual shadows: light (top-left, rgba(255,255,255,0.8)) + dark (bottom-right, rgba(0,0,0,0.15))

interaction.hover: shadow softens slightly; barely perceptible depth change
interaction.focus: inner shadow glow or color accent ring—must be visible for a11y
interaction.motion: smooth 200ms ease; the tactile press animation is core to the style
interaction.animation: pressed state: inset shadow (box-shadow inset) on active/click

dos: Use a single mid-range background color (not pure black or white)
Match shadows to the background hue (warm gray bg = warm shadows)
Use the pressed/inset style for active states to simulate physical feedback
Accent with a single vivid color for primary actions only
donts: Do not use on dark backgrounds—shadows are illegible
Do not use multiple accent colors—monochromatic is the rule
Do not apply to small elements (< 32px)—shadows become muddy
Do not combine with glassmorphism or hard shadows
Do not use pure white or pure black as the base—mid-grays only

name: Retro Terminal
description: CRT monitor aesthetic with phosphor glow, scanlines, and monospace type on dark backgrounds. Commands are first-class UI. Raw, authentic, and deeply nostalgic.

layout.stack: top-to-bottom text flow; output grows downward like a real terminal
layout.whitespace: blank lines as the separator—no margin/padding metaphors

typography.font_category: monospace always (JetBrains Mono, IBM Plex Mono, Courier Prime, VT323 for headers)
typography.scale: uniform or near-uniform: 13-16px throughout; 24-32px for status headers
typography.weight: 400 only; bold for prompts ($, >, #) and errors
typography.transform: lowercase for commands, UPPERCASE for system output/errors
typography.line_height: tight (1.3-1.4) to mimic terminal line density

shape.borders: 1px solid phosphor color; dashed for pending/waiting states

interaction.hover: cursor changes to text-cursor; color shifts from dim to bright phosphor
interaction.focus: blinking block cursor (|) or underscore; no outline ring
interaction.motion: instant for most; typewriter effect for output (character-by-character)
interaction.animation: scanline overlay (CSS animation), cursor blink, typing reveal

dos: Use phosphor color families: green (#00ff41), amber (#ffb000), white (#c8c8c8)
Add scanline overlay with CSS (repeating linear-gradient transparency)
Simulate CRT flicker subtly on page load
Prefix interactive elements with prompt symbols ($ for commands, > for input)
donts: Do not use sans-serif or serif fonts of any kind
Do not add images, icons, or any non-text visual elements
Do not use color beyond the phosphor palette and black
Do not add gradients, shadows, or card-based layouts
Do not use rounded corners on anything

name: Biomimetic
description: Inspired by natural systems—flowing curves, organic textures, growth patterns. Asymmetric layouts mirror natural irregularity. Earthy palettes and tactile surfaces.

layout.grid: fluid, asymmetric; content follows natural flow, not rigid columns
layout.stack: overlapping blobs, curved section dividers, spiral or radial arrangement
layout.density: medium-low; natural forms need room to breathe
layout.whitespace: irregular; mimics natural negative space (like gaps between leaves)

typography.font_category: organic serif or flowing humanist (Cormorant Garamond, Fraunces, Cabinet Grotesk)
typography.weight: light (300) for body, semibold (600) for emphasis, never heavy
typography.line_height: generous (1.7-1.8) mimicking the irregular spacing of handwritten text

shape.radius: organic: irregular SVG blobs for containers, 40-60% on UI components
shape.borders: subtle natural texture borders or no borders; organic shapes define edges
shape.shadows: warm, diffused (0 20px 60px rgba(brown,0.15)); like sunlight through leaves

interaction.hover: gentle morph—shape subtly shifts, colors warm up
interaction.focus: organic ring that follows the element's natural shape
interaction.motion: 300-500ms ease with spring physics; nothing moves in straight lines
interaction.animation: breathing animation (scale 1.0 → 1.02 → 1.0), gentle parallax on scroll

dos: Use SVG blob shapes instead of rectangles for section backgrounds
Apply subtle noise/grain texture overlay (CSS filter or SVG feTurbulence)
Use botanical or natural imagery with consistent desaturated treatment
Asymmetric layouts feel more natural—resist grid alignment
donts: Do not use sharp geometric shapes or hard-edged layouts
Do not use neon or highly saturated colors—nature is muted
Do not use monospace fonts
Do not create rigid symmetrical layouts
Do not use borders with right angles on primary containers

name: Aurora Gradient
description: Bold, cinematic gradients dominate every surface. Deep space backgrounds, luminous color bleeds, and neon accents create an immersive, otherworldly interface.

layout.grid: centered single or 2-column; hero sections span full viewport
layout.stack: full-bleed horizontal sections stacked vertically, no hard breaks
layout.density: low; elements float in vast gradient space
layout.whitespace: the gradient IS the whitespace; content islands in color

typography.font_category: display or futuristic sans (Clash Display, Bebas Neue, PP Neue Montreal, Space Grotesk)
typography.transform: all-caps or title case for hero; sentence for body

shape.radius: full pill (9999px) for buttons/badges; 0px for large containers
shape.borders: gradient borders: border with linear-gradient background-clip hack
shape.shadows: glow effects: 0 0 40px color, 0 0 80px color at lower opacity

interaction.hover: gradient shift + glow intensity increase; color rotation animation
interaction.motion: 300ms ease with slight overshoot; liquid-feeling transitions
interaction.animation: slow gradient animation (background-position shift or hue-rotate)

dos: Use multi-stop gradients (3-5 colors) for backgrounds and hero elements
Apply gradient mesh or radial spotlight effects as visual anchors
Use glassmorphism cards to maintain legibility over gradient backgrounds
Ensure text contrast ≥ 4.5:1 despite gradient backgrounds—test all positions
donts: Do not use more than 5 gradient colors in a single gradient
Do not use gradients on body text—only on display/hero text (clip-text)
Do not use pure black text on gradient backgrounds
Do not use this style for data-heavy or utility interfaces
Do not animate gradients on every element—choose one focal animation point
